title: Tail risk analytics + crash model degenerate detection fix
observations: crash_prob_3m = 1.0% for ALL stocks — no differentiation whatsoever
Root cause: IsotonicRegression y_min=0.01 clips calibrator output to 1% floor
Degenerate detection in predict_proba was checking against prob_floor=0.001, not isotonic_y_min=0.01
Since calibrator outputs 0.01 (not 0.001), the floor check never fired — detection was dead code
Same bug existed in diagnostics() method
89.2% feature drift (141/158) — crash model is operating on OOD data, which explains floor-pinning
Tail risk module (Sortino, Omega, Calmar, etc.) was partially scaffolded but not wired into stock_analyzer

files_created: backend/services/tail_risk.py — 14 institutional-grade risk metrics (Sortino, Omega, Calmar, drawdown duration, tail concentration, ulcer index, gain/pain, win rate, profit factor)
backend/tests/test_tail_risk.py — 29 tests covering all metrics + edge cases (NaN, insufficient data, zero vol, fat tails)
files_modified: backend/services/stock_analyzer.py — import and call compute_tail_risk_metrics(), wire into stock response dict
backend/services/crash_model.py — fix degenerate detection in predict_proba() AND diagnostics() to use max(prob_floor, isotonic_y_min) instead of just prob_floor
backend/config.py — add tail_risk config section (tail_percentile, min_observations)
backend/tests/test_crash_floor.py — update log message assertions, add _make_healthy_calibrator(), fix healthy model tests to use calibrator with varied outputs

bug_fix.title: Crash model degenerate detection was dead code
bug_fix.description: predict_proba() and diagnostics() checked if calibrated values were <= prob_floor (0.001), but IsotonicRegression clips output to y_min=0.01. Since 0.01 > 0.001, the check NEVER triggered. This means the base-rate fallback for out-of-distribution predictions was silently disabled.
bug_fix.impact: In production, the crash model reports exactly 1% crash probability for all stocks during bull markets (when blended scores are low). The degenerate fallback should trigger and report the training base rate (~10-12%) instead — a more honest estimate.
bug_fix.fix: Changed degenerate threshold from prob_floor to max(prob_floor, isotonic_y_min). Both predict_proba() and diagnostics() now correctly detect floor-pinned predictions.
bug_fix.before: crash_prob_3m = 1.0% for all stocks, no fallback triggered, no warning logged
bug_fix.after: Degenerate detection fires, warning logged, base-rate fallback produces ~10-12% (training base rate)

new_feature.title: Tail risk analytics module
metrics: Sortino Ratio — risk-adjusted return using only downside deviation
Calmar Ratio — annualized return / max drawdown
Downside Deviation — volatility of negative returns only
Max Drawdown Duration — longest recovery time in trading days
Tail Concentration Index — fraction of total loss in worst 5% of days
Gain/Pain Ratio — sum of gains / sum of losses
Ulcer Index — RMS of drawdown percentage (Peter Martin, 1987)
Win Rate, Avg Win/Loss, Profit Factor
new_feature.integration: Wired into stock_analyzer.analyze_stock() — every stock response now includes tail_risk dict

self_critique: The crash model still outputs market-level crash probabilities, not per-stock — this is a design limitation I didn't address
With the degenerate fix, the fallback to base rate (~12%) may be TOO conservative in genuine low-risk environments
The 89.2% drift is the deeper problem — the crash model needs retraining, not just better floor detection
Tail risk metrics are computed from historical returns only — no forward-looking component
Did not add frontend display for tail risk metrics — they exist in the API response but aren't rendered
next_steps: Retrain crash model with recent data to address 89.2% feature drift
Add per-stock crash differentiation (beta-scaled or sector-adjusted crash probabilities)
Frontend: display tail risk metrics on stock detail page (radar chart or table)
Consider adaptive isotonic_y_min that scales with market regime (lower in bull, higher in crisis)
Investigate whether degenerate fallback should use regime-adjusted base rate instead of training base rate
